transcript:
SOURCE: Fathom (via HubSpot internal notes) — https://fathom.video/share/gS7gKtJPWyfcLXsAa5boMiNjkuNvSkKg

AI Meeting Summary ✨

Generated by Fathom // View Recording (45 mins - No highlights)

Deal Information

Prospect

David Bliven, solo family law firm owner in Westchester County, NY (high-income market). Currently a solo attorney with two senior paralegals and two virtual assistants; hiring an associate in August and potentially another next year. Experiencing rapid growth vs. last year’s slump.

Call Context

David raised his hand specifically to explore SMB Team’s fractional COO/CFO-style program. He needs hands-on management support to scale, institute KPIs, improve intake conversion, reduce no‑shows, and implement objective staff accountability. He is comparing against a KPI dashboard tool (Law Firm KPI).

Seats

Fractional COO engagement (service seat: 1 firm). Future expansion: potential addition to Elite Coach after COO establishes quick wins.

Budget

Not explicitly stated. Prospect acknowledged significant ROI potential (e.g., +$20k–$40k/month without additional ad spend) and openness to proceed to agreement review on next call.

Current State

Marketing: On SMB Team full-service marketing + Virtual Video Growth (AI video). Leads flow into Clio Grow.

Operations: Solo attorney; two senior paralegals (salaried ~$97.5k and $105k); two VAs (stabilized since late January). No formal billable-hour minimums or KPI accountability. AR >$100k outstanding. Intake conversion ~15% with 40–73% no‑show rates in recent months.

Reporting: Some intake tracking shared with SMB CSM (Talia); lacks consistent closed-won metrics.

Pain Points

Intake performance:

~15% lead-to-client conversion; 40–73% consult no-show rates. Missed revenue without increasing marketing spend.

Management bandwidth:

Attorney doing lawyering + firm management; lacks time, systems, and confidence in KPI-driven leadership.

Lack of KPIs/accountability:

No objective benchmarks for intake, paralegals, or future associates; quarterly/annual reviews lack data.

Underutilized paralegals:

~10 billable hrs/week each at ~$300/hr; effectively treading water vs. salary/benefits; no collections focus.

Accounts receivable:

$100k AR; estimates ~$50k collectible; inconsistent retainer replenishment/collections process.

Stress/cash cushion:

Drained rainy-day fund; owner underpaying self; seeks stability and hiring runway.

Specific Requirements

Hands-on fractional COO:

Weekly 1:1 implementation to build KPIs, processes, accountability, compensation/bonus structures, and communications for team buy‑in.

Intake optimization:

Reduce no-shows, raise conversion to ≥20%; clear ownership of follow-ups; reporting on show rates, same-day close, and post-consult close.

Performance management:

Objective metrics and quarterly “Boulders” for intake, paralegals, and attorneys; bill/collect targets (e.g., 20+ hrs/wk baseline for paras).

Collections/AR:

System to ensure retainer replenishment and AR recovery workflows; owner visibility into AR aging/collectibility.

Reporting & dashboards:

Clio Grow/Manage reporting for total/qualified leads, consults set/completed, close rates, show rates, bill vs. collect, utilization, realization.

Tech Stack

Clio Grow + Clio Manage (leads integrated from SMB marketing)

SMB Team Full-Service Marketing + Virtual Video Growth (AI video)

Evaluating: Law Firm KPI (third-party dashboard tool)

Objections

Tool overlap vs. Law Firm KPI:

Concern about duplicative offerings. Addressed by positioning COO as implementation/management (vs. dashboards only); not fully closed but reframed.

Time constraints:

Prospect is overloaded with consults; concern about ability to attend group coaching. Addressed by recommending COO first; coaching later.

Budget sensitivity:

Not directly raised; implied past cash stress. Mitigated by ROI math showing $20k–$40k/month upside without more ad spend.

Other Solutions Under Evaluation

Law Firm KPI (dashboard pulling from Clio; claims experience with SMB clients)

Timeline

Immediate next step: Review recording/notes and COO agreement on Friday at 4:00 PM ET (Dan in CT). Associate hire slated for August; wants COO in place ahead of that.

Decision Maker

David Bliven (owner/solo attorney)

Procurement Process

Direct owner decision. Wants to review call recap and formal COO agreement; likely to proceed if ROI path and scope are clear.

Next steps

Dan to:

Send call recording and notes.

Calendar invite for Friday 4:00 PM ET.

Prepare and walk through fractional COO agreement.

Message Talia for recent intake tracking reports.

David to:

Eat! Then review notes/recording.

Pull/confirm key metrics (monthly closed-won, show rates, AR aging) before next call.

Q&A

Questions We Asked

What prompted your interest—AI or our fractional COO/CFO offering?

David: Fractional CFO/COO; needs help with management, KPIs, and intake.

What are current intake KPIs (conversion rate, no-shows)?

David: ~15% conversion; no-shows 40–73% (Sep–Dec data shared: Sep 68 set/30 completed; Oct 42/15; Nov 43/14; Dec 73% no-show).

How many new clients per month and average case values?

David: Currently ~6/month (was ~1/month last year). Initial retainer ~$10k; avg case value $20k–$30k.

What’s your tech stack (CRM/CM)?

David: Clio Grow + Manage; SMB marketing integrated leads into Grow.

Paralegal comp, bill rates, and billable hours?

David: Salaries ~$97.5k and $105k; bill at ~$270–$320/hr (avg $300); bill ~10 hrs/week each.

AR exposure and collectibility?

David: >$100k AR; estimates ~$50k collectible; many debtors have assets/on‑books jobs.

Desired outcome/1-year vision?

David: On track to $2.0–$2.5M by 2027; add office manager, 3 VAs, 3 paralegals, 1–2 associates; restore cash cushion; pay self properly.

Questions They Asked

How does SMB’s COO program differ from tools like Law Firm KPI, and do you offer staff training/coaching?

Dan: COO is hands-on weekly 1:1 implementation across intake, KPIs, ops, collections, hiring, and accountability communications—beyond dashboards. Coaching also available later (Elite Coach) for peer learning and owner development; recommend COO first given bandwidth and immediate ops needs.

Can we get Clio-based reporting and tie leads to outcomes?

Dan: Yes—leads already flow into Clio Grow; we’ll coordinate with Talia and use Clio reports + our tracker to expose total/qualified leads, consult outcomes, and follow-ups for conversion lift.

Action Items

Message Talia re: intake/consult metrics; request recent charts/data - WATCH (5 secs)
Send David COO agreement for Fri review - WATCH (5 secs)
Send David recording + notes of today's call - WATCH (5 secs)
Send David calendar invite for Fri 4 pm ET - WATCH (5 secs)
